user_outcome: A SwornAgent operator runs sworn capabilities or sworn models and sees truthful per-prefix reachability for keyless local servers, then runs sworn run with a local implementer model and the implementer leg reaches that server while capable configured models complete the structured roles.
rationale: S01 makes endpoints constructible; this slice makes them reachable through the loop and honest in enumeration. Local prefixes cannot share keyProbe because they have no key, and a preflight before each dispatch would be redundant and stale. Enumeration therefore uses a bounded models-list probe while dispatch relies on the real request. The local registry entry continues to declare all in-process roles so S04/S05 can later admit each model by capability rather than a provider-name allowlist. This slice owns contract C-03 and consumes C-01/C-04.
in_scope: Register ollama-cloud/ with the credentialed OAI chat identity and register ollama/, llamacpp/, lmstudio/, vllm/, and localai/ in a separate keyless OAI chat entry whose stable identity is oai-local-inprocess; derive both prefix groups from S01 endpoint descriptors rather than another literal prefix list.

The keyless local in-process identity declares implementer, verifier, and captain roles exactly like the shared OAI chat driver. This slice does not claim every local model has the structured capability needed for every role; S04/S05 performs that later model-level admission.

Implement a local reachability probe that performs GET <base_url>/models with a two-second total request timeout, no retries, no Authorization header, and availability only for HTTP 2xx plus a parseable OpenAI models object with a data array.

Run local endpoint probes concurrently during sworn capabilities so five unreachable defaults cost approximately one timeout window rather than five serial windows. Render each canonical prefix as reachable or unavailable with a secret/payload-safe reason; the command remains an enumeration and does not send chat completions.

Make sworn models use each configured local OAI /models endpoint and returned IDs instead of native Ollama /api/tags for ollama/; ollama-native/ retains the existing native catalog semantics if exposed by the command.

Thread the config-derived model.ProviderConfig/registry from cmd/sworn into capabilities, models, single-task run, release run, parallel startup resolution, and every RunSlice call. Production command paths must not fall back to a config-blind ProviderConfigFromEnv after config.Load has succeeded.

Add a command-level end-to-end fixture in which sworn run uses ollama/<model> for the implementer and capable configured fixture models for captain/verifier, exercises real HTTP transport plus the agent tool loop in a temporary git workspace, and reaches the verified/merge outcome with the implementer endpoint request recorded.

Add an opt-in live local-Ollama reachability path driven by an explicitly supplied local model ID. Record the real sworn run output/exit code in proof; if no suitable local daemon/model is available, record the exact skip reason rather than claiming coverage.

Produce the required mutation proof: route a local descriptor through keyProbe and observe unavailable with no key, then restore the reachability probe against a live httptest transport and observe available. Preserve both outputs in proof.json/journal.
out_of_scope: Endpoint descriptor construction, config schema, credential mapping, and ollama/ prefix migration are S01-endpoint-registry-config.
Endpoint dialect generation and runtime request shaping are S03-endpoint-dialect-conformance.
Nightly provider matrix orchestration is S04-live-provider-matrix.
Making a capability-insufficient local model serve captain/verifier is forbidden; S04/S05 owns capability discovery and eligibility, and sworn#86 owns subprocess captain support.
Caching availability or preflighting before each dispatch is excluded; the real dispatch request remains authoritative.

effort_complexity.rationale: This is a broad integration slice spanning registry identity, concurrent network probes, catalog behaviour, config propagation, and the full run command. Correctness depends on keeping advertised availability, role resolution, and actual dispatch aligned across single and parallel paths.

risks.description: A local prefix is accidentally registered behind keyProbe and is permanently unavailable despite a running daemon.
risks.mitigation: Keyless descriptors occupy their own reachability-probed registry entry, and the required mutation proof demonstrates that keyProbe fails before the corrected probe succeeds.

risks.description: Five unreachable local defaults make sworn capabilities block serially or leak response content in errors.
risks.mitigation: Use one two-second timeout per request, launch probes concurrently, collect results deterministically by prefix, and expose only classified reasons without bodies.

risks.description: Capabilities/models use configured endpoints while the actual loop silently rebuilds config from environment and dispatches to defaults.
risks.mitigation: Create the provider config/registry once after config.Load and inject it into every production run path; command tests set a non-default fixture URL and fail if any request escapes it.

risks.description: The proof says local sworn run works but only a leaf client or fake driver was exercised.
risks.mitigation: The deterministic test starts at the sworn run command and observes real HTTP traffic from the implementer tool loop in a temporary git repo; proof additionally records an opt-in real local Ollama run or an explicit skip.

acceptance_criteria.text: WHEN `sworn capabilities` runs with configured ollama/, llamacpp/, lmstudio/, vllm/, and localai/ endpoints THE SYSTEM SHALL concurrently GET each normalized `<base_url>/models` with a two-second total request timeout, no retries, and no Authorization header, and SHALL exit 0 after rendering exactly one stable prefix-sorted line per local endpoint. A 2xx JSON object whose `data` member is an array SHALL render `endpoint <prefix>/: reachable (<N> models)` including N=0; timeout, other transport error, non-2xx, invalid JSON, missing data, or non-array data SHALL render respectively `endpoint <prefix>/: unavailable (timeout|transport|http-status-<code>|invalid-json|missing-data|data-not-array)`. No response body, model payload, or credential SHALL be rendered, and five hanging fixtures SHALL complete in less than three seconds rather than five serial timeout windows (C-01, C-03, N-02).

WHEN registry.Default resolves ollama/, llamacpp/, lmstudio/, vllm/, or localai/ for implementer, verifier, or captain THE SYSTEM SHALL return the driver whose Name is exactly `oai-local-inprocess` without consulting an API key; ollama-cloud/ SHALL return the credentialed `oai-inprocess` entry. registry.Default SHALL obtain both prefix groups only from `model.EndpointDescriptors()`, filtering the descriptor CredentialProvider field as empty versus non-empty; `TestEndpointDerivedRegistryPrefixes` SHALL parse internal/driver/registry/registry.go with stdlib go/parser and fail if any of the six prefix string literals occurs there or if the registered prefix set differs from the filtered descriptor results (N-02).

WHEN `sworn models --provider <local-prefix>` receives HTTP 2xx JSON `data` records whose `id` values are non-empty strings THE SYSTEM SHALL deduplicate IDs, sort them lexically, print `<prefix>/ (<N> models)` followed by one `  <prefix>/<id>   tools: unknown` line per ID, and exit 0; an empty array SHALL print the zero-model header and exit 0. Timeout, other transport error, non-2xx, invalid JSON, missing data, non-array data, or any missing/empty/non-string id SHALL print exactly `<prefix>/: error: <timeout|transport|http-status-<code>|invalid-json|missing-data|data-not-array|invalid-model-id>` with no body/value, exit 1, and print no model rows (C-01, C-03, N-02).

WHEN config.Load succeeds, `configuredProviderRuntime(cfg)` in cmd/sworn/provider_config.go SHALL be called exactly once per capabilities, models, single-task run, release run, or parallel run invocation and SHALL return one model.ProviderConfig containing cfg.Providers overrides plus one `*registry.Registry` constructed from that ProviderConfig. capabilities and models SHALL receive that ProviderConfig; single-task/release run SHALL assign the exact registry pointer to run.Options.Registry and onward to RunSliceOptions.Registry; parallel startup resolution and every worker closure SHALL receive the same pointer. `TestProviderConfigFeedsEveryCommand` SHALL set ollama.base_url to a unique httptest URL, assert every command reaches it, inject a pointer-recording RunSlice seam for serial/parallel paths, assert one identical non-nil registry pointer at startup and every call, and fail if production command code calls model.ProviderConfigFromEnv or registry.Default outside configuredProviderRuntime (C-01, N-02).

WHEN `TestRunLocalImplementerReachability` invokes the compiled `sworn run --task 'create LOCAL_PROVIDER_PROOF.txt containing ok' --base main --implementer-model ollama/local-fixture --verifier-model openai-completions/verifier-fixture` in a temporary git workspace whose config sets captain.model=openai-completions/captain-fixture and no escalation models THE SYSTEM SHALL send exactly three ollama/local-fixture chat requests: turn 1 returns a `write` call for `LOCAL_PROVIDER_PROOF.txt`, turn 2 contains that tool result and returns a `read` call for the same file, and turn 3 contains both prior assistant/tool pairs and returns terminal text. The process SHALL exit 0; stderr SHALL contain a final line matching `sworn run: merged sworn/<branch> into main (PASS)`; git SHALL be on main with the feature tip an ancestor, LOCAL_PROVIDER_PROOF.txt SHALL contain exactly `ok`, and the single generated S01-task/status.json SHALL have state `verified` plus verification.result `pass`. The local fixture log SHALL contain model `local-fixture` on all three requests and zero requests bearing any other implementer model ID (C-04, N-02).

WHEN `TestLocalProbeMutationProof` runs for ollama/ with no key THE SYSTEM SHALL first replace its availability probe with keyProbe and assert the exact observation `ollama/: available=no reason=missing-key`, then restore endpointProbe against a live HTTP server returning `data:[{id:"local-fixture"}]` and assert `ollama/: available=yes models=1`. The implementer SHALL copy both lines under `## AC-06 mutation proof` in docs/release/2026-07-14-local-cloud-providers/S02-keyless-loop-dispatch/journal.md, and docs/release/2026-07-14-local-cloud-providers/S02-keyless-loop-dispatch/proof.json `reachability.evidence` SHALL contain `AC-06 mutation proof — journal.md — go test ./internal/driver/registry -run '^TestLocalProbeMutationProof$'` so both outcomes are durable and verifier-addressable (N-02).

THE IMPLEMENTER SHALL evaluate live preconditions in this order and stop at the first failure: empty/absent SWORN_LIVE_LOCAL_MODEL=`SKIP missing-local-model`; value not matching `ollama/<non-empty-id>`=`SKIP invalid-local-model`; empty/absent SWORN_LIVE_VERIFIER_MODEL=`SKIP missing-verifier-model`; empty config.json captain.model, registry.Resolve(captain.model, RoleCaptain) error, or that resolved registry entry's availability probe returning false=`SKIP captain-unavailable`; normalized configured ollama `/models` not returning 2xx parseable data within two seconds=`SKIP endpoint-unreachable`; data lacking the exact `<non-empty-id>`=`SKIP model-not-listed`. For a run, create a disposable temporary git repository on branch main with user identity configured and one empty initial commit, use it as the command working directory, point SWORN_CONFIG_PATH at a temporary config carrying the selected captain/verifier and ollama base, and invoke the built binary as `sworn run --task 'create LOCAL_PROVIDER_PROOF.txt containing ok' --base main --implementer-model <literal-local-model-id> --verifier-model <literal-verifier-model-id>`; any subsequent failure SHALL fail rather than skip and the release worktree SHALL remain unchanged. docs/release/2026-07-14-local-cloud-providers/S02-keyless-loop-dispatch/journal.md `## AC-07 live local Ollama` SHALL record that exact command with literal non-secret model IDs, `captain.model=<literal-model-id>`, endpoint scheme/host without userinfo/query, initial/final temporary-repository commit IDs, final `sworn run: merged sworn/<branch> into main (PASS)` stderr line, generated status state/result, and exit 0; it SHALL contain no credential, request/response body, prompt, tool content, or other model output. On preflight failure the section SHALL contain exactly one line using `-` for unavailable fields: `SKIP <first-code> local_model=<literal-or-> verifier_model=<literal-or-> captain_model=<literal-or-> endpoint=<scheme-host-or->`; fields SHALL contain a literal only if its ordered check already passed before the failure. The matching proof.json `reachability.evidence` SHALL contain `AC-07 live local Ollama — journal.md`; a unit test SHALL NOT be represented as live evidence (N-02).

THE SYSTEM SHALL preserve existing driver conformance, registry, catalog, run, and command contracts: `go test ./internal/driver/... ./internal/model/... ./internal/run/... ./cmd/sworn/...` and `go vet ./...` SHALL exit 0, and `gofmt -l internal/driver internal/model internal/run cmd/sworn` SHALL exit 0 with empty stdout (N-02).
